title: 10 best herbal teas for joint pain
intro_desc: Herbal teas for joint pain are infusions made from various plants and herbs that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to alleviate joint pain and inflammation. Examples of such herbs include ginger, turmeric, green tea, and willow bark. Ginger and turmeric contain compounds called gingerols and curcumin, which inhibit the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body. Green tea is rich in antioxidants that reduce inflammation, while willow bark contains salicin, a natural pain reliever. Drinking these teas can improve the quality of life for those suffering from joint pain, allowing them to move more freely, engage in physical activities, and reduce their reliance on prescription medications.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black alder tea helps with joint pain because it contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. This natural substance found in the bark and leaves of the black alder tree may help reduce swelling, stiffness, and discomfort in the joints. Additionally, black alder tea is rich in antioxidants and other bioactive compounds that support overall joint health, making it a popular herbal remedy for individuals seeking relief from joint pain and inflammation.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Black alder tea has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which are crucial for joint pain as they help reduce swelling, redness, and pain in the affected area. This is mainly due to the presence of salicylates, flavonoids, and tannins.
Analgesic: The tea acts as a natural painkiller, providing relief from joint pain. This is attributed to the salicin content, which is a precursor to aspirin.

Diuretic: Black alder tea promotes diuresis, helping to eliminate excess fluids from the body. This is important for joint pain as it aids in reducing fluid buildup and inflammation in the joints, thereby alleviating discomfort.

Antioxidant: The tea contains high levels of antioxidants, which help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals. This property is essential for joint pain as it supports joint health by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.

Circulation-enhancing: Black alder tea improves blood circulation, ensuring that nutrients and oxygen reach the joints efficiently. This is important for joint pain as it supports tissue repair and reduces stiffness in the joints.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried black alder bark and rinse it under cold water to remove any impurities.
Place the bark in a pot and add 1 cup of cold water, allowing it to soak for 30 minutes to release the beneficial compounds.
After soaking, bring the pot to a boil and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the bark and allow it to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Consume 2-3 cups of the black alder tea daily, as needed, for relief of joint pain.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the black alder plant contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate joint pain.
Stems: The stems of the black alder plant contain compounds that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the joints.
Leaves: The leaves of the black alder plant are rich in antioxidants that can help reduce joint pain and inflammation.
Flowers: The flowers of the black alder plant contain natural oils that can help soothe sore joints and alleviate pain.
Seeds: The seeds of the black alder plant are a good source of omega-3 fatty acids, which can help reduce inflammation and joint pain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Celery tea helps with joint pain because it contains a compound called apigenin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. Joint pain is often caused by inflammation in the body, and apigenin can help reduce this inflammation, thereby alleviating joint pain. Additionally, celery tea is also rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals. This protection can further help reduce inflammation and joint pain. Furthermore, celery tea contains a variety of essential minerals, such as potassium, magnesium, and sodium, which are important for maintaining healthy joints. These minerals can help improve joint mobility and reduce pain and stiffness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Celery tea contains polyacetylene compounds that help reduce inflammation in the joints, providing relief from pain and swelling.
Analgesic: The apigenin in celery tea acts as a natural painkiller, helping to alleviate joint pain and discomfort.
Diuretic: Celery tea promotes diuresis, which helps remove fluids and toxins from the body, reducing pressure on joints and alleviating pain.

Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content, including luteolin and quercetin, in celery tea helps protect joints from free radical damage and reduce oxidative stress, thereby reducing joint pain and inflammation.
Alkalizing: Celery tea has an alkalizing effect on the body, which helps balance pH levels and reduce acidity, thus reducing joint pain and stiffness.
remedy_recipe: Chop one cup of fresh celery leaves and stems, then rinse them thoroughly.
Place the chopped celery in a pot and add four cups of water.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, then add honey or lemon to taste.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day for best results.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Celery seeds contain a compound called apigenin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate joint pain.
Stems: Celery stems contain a variety of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce joint inflammation and pain.
Leaves: Celery leaves are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects and can help reduce joint pain.
Roots: Celery roots contain a compound called polyacetylenes, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects and can help reduce joint pain.
Flowers: Celery flowers contain essential oils that have anti-inflammatory properties and can help alleviate joint pain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kinnikinnick tea helps with joint pain because it contains several anti-inflammatory compounds, such as tannins and essential oils, that can reduce swelling and inflammation in the joints. Additionally, it is rich in antioxidants that help to protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to joint pain and inflammation. Furthermore, kinnikinnick tea has been traditionally used by indigenous peoples as a natural remedy for arthritis and other joint-related conditions, due to its pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory properties. Drinking kinnikinnick tea regularly may help to alleviate joint pain, improve mobility, and enhance overall joint health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Kinnikinnick tea helps reduce joint pain by decreasing inflammation, thanks to the presence of tannins and flavonoids.
Analgesic: It alleviates pain by acting as a natural analgesic, containing compounds such as gallic acid and ellagic acid.
Antioxidant: Its high antioxidant content, including vitamins A, C, and E, helps protect joints from damage caused by free radicals.
Circulation-boosting: Kinnikinnick tea promotes blood flow, ensuring nutrients reach joint tissues and aiding in joint repair, due to the presence of essential oils.
Immune-supporting: By strengthening the immune system, it helps combat joint inflammation and pain, as many joint issues stem from autoimmune responses.
remedy_recipe: In a dry pan, gently heat 2 tablespoons of dried willow bark, 1 tablespoon of dried licorice root, and 1 tablespoon of dried black cohosh for 5 minutes to release their essential oils.
Transfer the heated herbs to a glass jar and add 1 quart of boiling water, then let it steep for 15-20 minutes with a lid.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a teapot or mug, discarding the solid herbs.
Stir in 1-2 teaspoons of honey or maple syrup for sweetness, if desired.
Drink 1 cup of the warm tea, 3 times a day, for up to a week to help alleviate joint pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in medicinal properties, the roots of kinnikinnick plant are used to make potent teas for joint pain relief.
Stems: High in anti-inflammatory compounds, kinnikinnick stems are often used in teas to soothe inflamed joints.
Leaves: Abundant in antioxidants, kinnikinnick leaves are used in teas to reduce oxidative stress and alleviate joint pain.
Flowers: Packed with essential oils, kinnikinnick flowers are used in teas to improve circulation and reduce joint inflammation.
Seeds: Containing beneficial nutrients, kinnikinnick seeds are used in teas to support joint health and mobility.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tea helps with joint pain because it is rich in vitamin C and other antioxidants that can reduce inflammation and swelling in the joints. The capsaicin found in bell peppers also has pain-relieving properties, which can help alleviate discomfort and improve joint mobility. Additionally, bell pepper tea is a natural source of minerals such as potassium, magnesium, and manganese, which are essential for maintaining healthy bones and connective tissues. Drinking bell pepper tea regularly can help reduce joint pain, improve joint function, and promote overall joint health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Bell pepper tea contains high levels of vitamin C and antioxidants, which help reduce inflammation in the joints, providing relief from pain and stiffness.
Pain relief: The capsaicin found in bell peppers activates pain receptors and depletes a neurotransmitter called substance P, which reduces the perception of pain.
Cartilage protection: Bell pepper tea is rich in vitamin C and bioflavonoids that support collagen production, essential for maintaining cartilage health and preventing joint degeneration.
Improved circulation: The capsaicin in bell peppers dilates blood vessels and improves blood flow, delivering essential nutrients and oxygen to joint tissues, promoting healing and reducing pain.

Antioxidant properties: Bell pepper tea contains several antioxidants, including flavonoids, carotenoids, and vitamin C, that combat oxidative stress and inflammation, contributing to overall joint health and reducing the risk of joint damage.
remedy_recipe: In a mortar, grind 1 teaspoon each of dried chamomile flowers, dried rosehips, and crushed bell pepper seeds.
Transfer the ground herbs to a tea infuser and place it in a teapot.
Pour 2 cups of boiling water over the herbs and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser and discard the herbs.
Drink the herbal tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, for joint pain relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, bell pepper leaves can help alleviate joint pain and inflammation.
Seeds: Packed with essential oils, bell pepper seeds can help reduce joint pain and improve mobility.
Stems: Containing high levels of vitamins and minerals, bell pepper stems can help strengthen joints and reduce pain.
Flowers: With natural pain-relieving properties, bell pepper flowers can help soothe joint pain and promote healing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Safflower tea helps with joint pain because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, such as safflower yellow and safflower yellow B. These compounds work to reduce inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate joint pain and stiffness. Additionally, safflower tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals and reduce oxidative stress. This can help reduce inflammation and promote overall joint health. Furthermore, safflower tea has been shown to improve blood circulation, which can help bring nutrients and oxygen to the joints, promoting healing and reducing pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Safflower tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of safflower yellow pigments and flavonoids, help alleviate joint pain by reducing inflammation and swelling in the affected area.
Analgesic: The analgesic effect of safflower tea, attributed to the alkaloids and saponins found in safflower, helps manage joint pain by blocking pain signals and providing relief.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content in safflower tea, provided by phenolic acids and vitamin E, helps with joint pain by neutralizing free radicals and reducing oxidative stress in the joints.

Circulation-enhancing: Safflower tea's ability to improve blood circulation, due to its richness in linoleic acid, helps alleviate joint pain by increasing the supply of nutrients and oxygen to the joints.

Immune-boosting: The immune-boosting properties of safflower tea, attributed to its polysaccharides and vitamins, help with joint pain by strengthening the immune system, which aids in preventing and managing joint-related conditions.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried safflower flowers, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the safflower flowers.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day for joint pain relief.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Safflower seeds contain active compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate joint pain.
Flowers: Safflower flowers are rich in antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation and provide relief from joint pain.
Leaves: Safflower leaves have been used in traditional medicine to treat arthritis and other joint-related conditions due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Safflower stems contain compounds that can improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation, providing relief from joint pain.
Roots: Safflower roots have been used in traditional medicine to treat joint pain due to their ability to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Shagbark hickory tea helps with joint pain because it contains several beneficial compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, that have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds work to reduce inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate joint pain and stiffness. Additionally, shagbark hickory tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the body. This can further help to reduce inflammation and improve joint health. Drinking shagbark hickory tea regularly may help to manage joint pain and promote overall joint health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Shagbark hickory tea contains compounds such as tannins and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation in the joints, providing relief from pain and swelling.
Analgesic: The tea has natural pain-relieving properties due to the presence of compounds like quercetin and kaempferol, which inhibit pain signals and help manage joint pain.
Antioxidant: Shagbark hickory tea is rich in antioxidants, which help protect joints from oxidative stress and damage, promoting joint health and reducing pain.
Circulation-enhancing: The tea helps improve blood circulation, ensuring that nutrients and oxygen reach the joints more efficiently, promoting joint health and reducing pain.

Immune-boosting: Shagbark hickory tea contains vitamins and minerals that support the immune system, which is crucial for managing joint pain as a strong immune system can help reduce inflammation and promote overall joint health.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 shagbark hickory bark pieces (each about 8-10 inches long) and rinse them thoroughly to remove dirt.
Chop the bark into small pieces, place them in a pot, and add 4 cups of water.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat, strain the tea into a cup, and let it cool down for 3-5 minutes.
Drink the tea (up to 3 cups per day) for joint pain relief, storing any leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the shagbark hickory plant contains anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate joint pain.
Leaves: The leaves of the shagbark hickory plant have been used in teas for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, providing relief for joint pain.
Seeds: The seeds of the shagbark hickory plant contain bioactive compounds that can help reduce inflammation and provide pain relief for joints.
Stems: The stems of the shagbark hickory plant contain medicinal properties that can help alleviate joint pain and reduce inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinnamon tea helps with joint pain because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the body, which is a common cause of joint pain. The compound cinnamaldehyde found in cinnamon has been shown to inhibit the release of arachidonic acid, a compound that triggers inflammation. Additionally, cinnamon is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the body from free radicals, which can cause damage to cells and tissues, including those in the joints. Drinking cinnamon tea may also help improve circulation, bringing more oxygen and nutrients to the joints, which can help alleviate pain and promote overall joint health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cinnamon tea contains active constituents like cinnamaldehyde and eugenol, which help reduce inflammation in the joints, providing relief from pain and stiffness.
Analgesic: The tea has natural pain-relieving properties due to the presence of compounds such as cinnamaldehyde and coumarin, helping to alleviate joint pain and discomfort.
Antioxidant: Cinnamon tea is rich in antioxidants, such as polyphenols and flavonoids, which help protect joints from oxidative stress and damage, reducing inflammation and promoting joint health.

Immune-boosting: The tea supports the immune system, thanks to its content of vitamins, minerals, and essential oils. A stronger immune system can help manage joint pain and inflammation more effectively.
Circulation-enhancing: Cinnamon tea helps improve blood circulation, allowing better nutrient delivery to joint tissues and promoting faster healing and reduced inflammation.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 1 cup of water for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried cinnamon bark and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the cinnamon pieces.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of raw honey (optional) and stir until dissolved.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day for joint pain relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the cinnamon plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate joint pain.
Rhizomes: Cinnamon rhizomes are rich in antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the joints.
Bark: The bark of the cinnamon plant contains cinnamaldehyde, which has been shown to reduce joint pain and inflammation.
Leaves: Cinnamon leaves are used in tea to help reduce inflammation and provide relief from joint pain.
Seeds: Cinnamon seeds contain a compound called eugenol, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate joint pain.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass tea helps with joint pain because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds, such as citral and geraniol, which can reduce inflammation in the body and alleviate pain in the joints. Additionally, lemongrass is rich in antioxidants, which can help to protect cells from damage and reduce oxidative stress, a common factor in chronic inflammation. Drinking lemongrass tea may also help to improve circulation, bringing more oxygen and nutrients to the joints and promoting overall joint health. Furthermore, lemongrass has been shown to have analgesic properties, which can help to relieve pain and discomfort in the joints. Overall, the anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and analgesic properties of lemongrass make it a natural and effective remedy for joint pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemongrass tea contains citral and geraniol, which have been shown to reduce inflammation, helping to alleviate joint pain and swelling.
Analgesic: Lemongrass tea has pain-relieving properties due to the presence of eugenol, making it effective in managing joint pain.
Antioxidant: Lemongrass tea contains powerful antioxidants that help protect joints from oxidative stress and damage, reducing the progression of joint pain.
Circulation-boosting: Lemongrass tea promotes blood circulation, ensuring that nutrients reach the joints more efficiently, aiding in joint tissue repair and reducing pain.
Detoxifying: Lemongrass tea aids in detoxifying the body, eliminating harmful toxins that can cause inflammation and joint pain, contributing to overall joint health.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried lemongrass; bring to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove pot from heat, add 1 teaspoon of turmeric powder and 1 teaspoon of grated ginger, then let it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solids.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of honey and a squeeze of lemon juice, if desired, for taste.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day for joint pain relief.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Lemongrass tea for joint pain is often made with rhizomes because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and swelling.
Leaves: The leaves of lemongrass contain essential oils and antioxidants that can help alleviate joint pain and inflammation.
Stems: Lemongrass stems are rich in citral, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful for joint pain relief.
Seeds: Lemongrass seeds are a good source of essential oils and have been used in traditional medicine to treat joint pain and inflammation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tea helps with joint pain because it contains high levels of silica, a mineral that plays a crucial role in the production and maintenance of collagen, a key component of joint cartilage. Regular consumption of horsetail tea can help to strengthen connective tissues, reduce inflammation and improve overall joint health. Additionally, horsetail is rich in antioxidants that can help to neutralize free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and promoting tissue repair in the joints. The anti-inflammatory properties of horsetail tea can help to alleviate pain and stiffness, making it an effective natural remedy for joint pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Horsetail tea helps reduce joint pain by decreasing inflammation, thanks to the presence of anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids.
Analgesic: Horsetail tea has pain-relieving properties that can alleviate joint pain, due to the presence of compounds like silica, which may help reduce pain sensitivity.

Collagen synthesis: Horsetail tea promotes collagen production, which is essential for maintaining joint health and reducing joint pain, as it contains silica, a mineral that supports collagen synthesis.

Diuretic: Horsetail tea helps eliminate excess fluids from the body, reducing swelling and inflammation in the joints, due to its natural diuretic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of equisetin and other saponins.

Antioxidant: Horsetail tea contains antioxidants that help protect joint tissues from damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of joint pain and inflammation, as a result of the high antioxidant content, including flavonoids and phenolic acids.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried horsetail herb.
Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool for a minute.
Steep the horsetail herb in the hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herb.
Drink the tea, up to three times a day, for joint pain relief.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Horsetail rhizomes are rich in silica, which is beneficial for joint health and can help alleviate joint pain.
Stems: Horsetail stems contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful for reducing joint inflammation and pain.
Leaves: Horsetail leaves are a rich source of antioxidants that can help reduce oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to joint pain and inflammation.
Seeds: Horsetail seeds contain essential minerals and nutrients that support overall joint health, including calcium, magnesium, and potassium.
Buds: Horsetail buds are harvested for their high concentration of bioactive compounds, which have been shown to have a positive effect on joint health and pain relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with joint pain because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce inflammation in the body, including the joints. This is due to the presence of compounds such as anethole, fenchone, and limonene, which are known to have anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, fennel tea contains antioxidants that can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to joint pain and inflammation. Drinking fennel tea regularly may help alleviate joint pain and improve overall joint health. However, it's important to note that while fennel tea can be a helpful complementary therapy for joint pain, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment or advice from a healthcare professional.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce joint pain and swelling, thanks to the presence of volatile oils like anethole, fenchone, and limonene.
Analgesic: Fennel tea acts as a natural painkiller, easing joint pain by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause pain and inflammation.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of fennel tea, due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect joints from oxidative stress and damage, reducing joint pain and promoting mobility.
Diuretic: Fennel tea's diuretic effect helps eliminate excess fluids and toxins from the body, reducing pressure on joints and alleviating joint pain.
Promotes digestion: Fennel tea aids in digestion and prevents gastric spasms, which can indirectly contribute to joint pain by reducing systemic inflammation and improving overall gut health.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water for 5 minutes.
Add 2 teaspoons of crushed fennel seeds to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the fennel seeds.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day for joint pain relief.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds contain volatile oils, such as anethole, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate joint pain.
Leaves: Fennel leaves are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce joint inflammation and pain.
Stems: Fennel stems contain essential oils, such as fenchone and limonene, which have pain-relieving properties and can help manage joint pain.
Flowers: Fennel flowers are a natural source of vitamin C and other antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and promote joint health.
Rhizomes: Fennel rhizomes contain a compound called anisic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, making it useful for managing joint pain.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat joint pain with herbal teas is by consuming regularly brewed teas made from potent anti-inflammatory herbs, such as ginger, turmeric, and green tea. These herbs have been scientifically proven to reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating joint pain. Additionally, willow bark tea, which contains salicin, a natural pain-relieving compound, can also provide significant relief. It's important to note that while herbal teas can complement traditional treatments, they should not replace any medication prescribed by a healthcare professional. Always consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment regimen.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to joint pain that are treated with herbal teas include arthritis, inflammation, and muscle pain. For instance, ginger tea is known to reduce inflammation and alleviate joint pain, while turmeric tea contains curcumin, which has strong anti-inflammatory properties. Willow bark tea has been used traditionally to treat arthritis and other types of pain due to its salicin content, similar to aspirin. Peppermint tea, on the other hand, can help soothe muscle aches and pains due to its menthol content, which has a cooling and calming effect. However, it is essential to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new herbal remedy.
